telemetry.offByDefault: Off by default. When on, the app records anonymous feature-usage counts only — never document content, file paths, or personal information. In this version nothing leaves your computer (counts stay in a local buffer you can inspect).
telemetry.auditable: Nothing has left your computer — these counts live only in memory and you can clear them at any time.

update.placeholder: Auto-update checks GitHub releases; the publish target is a placeholder until the project is published — updates will not function until a real release channel is configured. The update client is wired and ready.
update.codeSigning: Applying updates in production requires a code-signing certificate. Acquiring the certificate is a manual real-world step documented in the code-signing workflow; until then, downloaded updates cannot be applied.

platform.macLinuxUnverified: macOS and Linux build configurations are included but UNVERIFIED on real hardware. Native modules (database, canvas) may fail to load on these platforms until a maintainer verifies on a real host.

locale.proofSample: Spanish (es-ES) is a translation sample to prove the localization framework works — some strings may remain in English. It is not a complete professional translation.

a11y.auditedWithGaps: Keyboard navigation and screen-reader (Windows Narrator) support cover the critical paths. Freehand annotation drawing and some canvas interactions remain pointer-centric — documented as known gaps.

ocr.notPublicationQuality: OCR produces best-effort recognized text, not a publication-quality transcription. Accuracy depends on scan quality; always verify against the original.

export.lossyReconstruction: Office and image exports are a best-effort reconstruction of the PDF's visual content, not a perfect round-trip. Complex layouts, fonts, and vector art may differ from the source.

sign.notLegalAdvice: A digital signature proves the file was not altered after signing; it is not legal advice and does not by itself make a document legally binding.
